channel.title: ConnectBlue Political Ad Tech News
channel.description: Latest developments in political advertising technology, campaign strategy, and ad tech trends shaping the 2026 election cycle.

item.title: Here's How Artificial Intelligence Is Shaping This Election Season
item.description: Florida Lt. Gov. Collins shared AI-generated video attack ad; 90% of LinkedIn/Facebook posts are AI-generated; Florida's AI disclosure law has enforcement gaps. Campaigns ARE using AI ads already—the competitive disadvantage is being AI-free, not being AI-native.
item.ns0:encoded: Florida Lt. Gov. Collins shared AI-generated video attack ad; 90% of LinkedIn/Facebook posts are AI-generated; Florida's AI disclosure law has enforcement gaps. Campaigns ARE using AI ads already—the competitive disadvantage is being AI-free, not being AI-native.]]>

item.title: Senate Revives Push for AI Labels in Political Ads
item.description: Senators Schatz, Curtis, and Warner introduced the AI Labeling Act requiring visible labels and machine-readable disclosures on AI-generated political content. FTC + state AGs + private parties gain enforcement power. Campaigns must plan for mandatory AI disclosure by fall.
item.ns0:encoded: Senators Schatz, Curtis, and Warner introduced the AI Labeling Act requiring visible labels and machine-readable disclosures on AI-generated political content. FTC + state AGs + private parties gain enforcement power. Campaigns must plan for mandatory AI disclosure by fall.]]>

item.title: Study Finds AI-Generated Videos Influence Voters as Effectively as Authentic Political Content
item.description: New UVU research shows AI deepfakes persuade voters at identical rates to authentic video—but voters only detect them 15-19% of the time. Media literacy provides zero protection. This upends campaign messaging strategy for 2026.

item.title: Tech Oversight Project Launches Public AI Super PAC Research Book and Ad Library
item.description: $65M+ from 17 Big Tech-backed super PACs now visible—campaigns must understand which AI companies are funding opposition and how they're spending to shape the 2026 midterms. This is table stakes for competitive intelligence.
item.ns0:encoded: $65M+ from 17 Big Tech-backed super PACs now visible—campaigns must understand which AI companies are funding opposition and how they're spending to shape the 2026 midterms. This is table stakes for competitive intelligence.]]>

item.title: A Network of Fake Pennsylvania Facebook Pages—Dark Money Funds Deceptive Messaging at Scale
item.description: Investigation documents Real Voices Media's 400+ page network operating across competitive states, funded through four-layer dark money chain: anonymous donors → Story Network Foundation → Commonwealth Communications → Next Level Digital. Pages (Pennsylvania Moms, Black PA) masquerade as community spaces while running coordinated political ads. Critical lesson: deceptive messaging infrastructure is operating at massive scale; campaigns must understand competitive dark money tactics.
item.ns0:encoded: Investigation documents Real Voices Media's 400+ page network operating across competitive states, funded through four-layer dark money chain: anonymous donors → Story Network Foundation → Commonwealth Communications → Next Level Digital. Pages (Pennsylvania Moms, Black PA) masquerade as community spaces while running coordinated political ads. Critical lesson: deceptive messaging infrastructure is operating at massive scale; campaigns must understand competitive dark money tactics.]]>

item.title: Spotwise Launches Real-Time Political Radio Ad Monitoring Platform
item.description: Spotwise.ai launches free real-time political radio ad tracking platform in Latvia, expanding to 14 European markets. Provides AI-powered ad transcripts, competitor spend tracking, placement monitoring. Signals growing market demand for ad tech transparency and competitive intelligence tools for campaigns.

item.title: AI-Funded PACs Dominate Midterm Ad Spend—$38M+ From OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic-backed PACs spent $38M+ in 2026 midterms supporting pro-AI regulation candidates, tilted toward Democrats. Marks first time major tech companies became dominant political ad players. Campaigns now compete not just with traditional opponents but with tech giants funding messaging directly.
item.ns0:encoded: OpenAI and Anthropic-backed PACs spent $38M+ in 2026 midterms supporting pro-AI regulation candidates, tilted toward Democrats. Marks first time major tech companies became dominant political ad players. Campaigns now compete not just with traditional opponents but with tech giants funding messaging directly.]]>

item.title: 2026 Election Ad Spending Set to Surge to $11.6 Billion
item.description: AdImpact revised 2026 political ad spend upward to $11.6B—surpassing 2024 presidential cycle and tracking 46% ahead of pace. Record spending means inventory constraints, premium pricing, and compressed timeline. Early movers avoid premium CPMs and placement delays.
item.ns0:encoded: AdImpact revised 2026 political ad spend upward to $11.6B—surpassing 2024 presidential cycle and tracking 46% ahead of pace. Record spending means inventory constraints, premium pricing, and compressed timeline. Early movers avoid premium CPMs and placement delays.]]>

item.title: The Pro-Israel Super PAC Cinematic Universe
item.description: United Democracy Project ($38M+) and Democratic Majority for Israel ($2.7M) deployed ~$8M through 9 shell PACs to obscure funding sources. Transfers made AFTER primaries voted, hiding true donors. Strategic insight: Super PACs using established partner organizations (EMILYs List, EDW, Blue Dog) as pass-throughs to hide influence on factional battles within Democratic Party.
item.ns0:encoded: United Democracy Project ($38M+) and Democratic Majority for Israel ($2.7M) deployed ~$8M through 9 shell PACs to obscure funding sources. Transfers made AFTER primaries voted, hiding true donors. Strategic insight: Super PACs using established partner organizations (EMILYs List, EDW, Blue Dog) as pass-throughs to hide influence on factional battles within Democratic Party.]]>

item.title: Washington Court Sides Against Meta In Battle Over Political Ads
item.description: $35M fine upheld: Washington Supreme Court ruled Meta must disclose political ad details on demand (cost, targeting, impressions, audience). Meta previously self-imposed political ad ban rather than comply. For campaigns: State-level ad transparency laws are becoming enforcement mechanism. Campaigns must prepare for granular disclosure requirements across multiple states.
item.ns0:encoded: $35M fine upheld: Washington Supreme Court ruled Meta must disclose political ad details on demand (cost, targeting, impressions, audience). Meta previously self-imposed political ad ban rather than comply. For campaigns: State-level ad transparency laws are becoming enforcement mechanism. Campaigns must prepare for granular disclosure requirements across multiple states.]]>

item.title: Horizon and Smartly Team Up
item.description: Horizon's AI-powered audience intelligence (HorizonOS Blu) now activates directly into Smartly's campaign workflows. Early results: 2x creative engagement lift, 35% reduction in cost-per-order. For political campaigns: unified audience data flowing directly to activation platforms eliminates siloed data friction. This is now table stakes for efficient media buying.
item.ns0:encoded: Horizon's AI-powered audience intelligence (HorizonOS Blu) now activates directly into Smartly's campaign workflows. Early results: 2x creative engagement lift, 35% reduction in cost-per-order. For political campaigns: unified audience data flowing directly to activation platforms eliminates siloed data friction. This is now table stakes for efficient media buying.]]>

item.title: FEC filings confirm GOP meddling in Dem primaries
item.description: Conservative Americans PAC funneled $4.3M+ through two pop-up super PACs (Real Change, Lead Left) to support 'less electable' Democratic primary candidates. GOP strategy: target open Democratic primaries in competitive districts to boost weaker nominees. Campaigns must expect coordinated cross-party interference in competitive primaries.
item.ns0:encoded: Conservative Americans PAC funneled $4.3M+ through two pop-up super PACs (Real Change, Lead Left) to support 'less electable' Democratic primary candidates. GOP strategy: target open Democratic primaries in competitive districts to boost weaker nominees. Campaigns must expect coordinated cross-party interference in competitive primaries.]]>

item.title: aKillion and Subtext Form a Strategic Partnership and Introduce IONCandidate
item.description: IONCandidate delivers 4.7x higher SMS response rates than industry benchmarks while automating voter file enrichment in real-time. AI-powered candidate persona responds immediately to voter texts; email outperforms benchmarks by 700% when integrated with SMS. Campaigns now have scalable voter intelligence without manual polling overhead.

item.title: As AIPAC spending surges, it faces a big primary test
item.description: AIPAC's United Democracy Project hits $38M+ spending—surpassing 2022 totals and setting new records. Dark money shell PACs obscure 40%+ of independent expenditures. For campaigns: Super PAC spending is becoming the defining force in Democratic primaries. Expect unprecedented outside spending to dominate down-ballot races through 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: AIPAC's United Democracy Project hits $38M+ spending—surpassing 2022 totals and setting new records. Dark money shell PACs obscure 40%+ of independent expenditures. For campaigns: Super PAC spending is becoming the defining force in Democratic primaries. Expect unprecedented outside spending to dominate down-ballot races through 2026.]]>
